evidence_quotes.quote: With the cost of purchasing video games, whether digital or physical, it should, by definition, be my property.
which may also no longer be used when a game has been permanently disabled by the forced implementation of ‘Always Online’.

paragraphs.text: I believe that my purchases, to which I have spent money, belong to me if they are not a danger to society. It would be unfair if a large, often US company, is willing to divest me of my purchase without compensation, with often inadequate information on why their lack of support and action is the end of my ability to make use of it. With the cost of purchasing video games, whether digital or physical, it should, by definition, be my property. All in all, as in the case of online video games, it is also about possible further Downloadable Content (DLC) which may also no longer be used when a game has been permanently disabled by the forced implementation of ‘Always Online’. Nor do I buy a car with the idea that if the company no longer wants to support them, it should stop operating.
